string.text:
%c[0,153,255,255]Ashot%c[0,140,140,140] is Freedom's cheerful and optimistic trader at Yanov station.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
According to himself, because of his "low prices and quality equipment", he has supposedly been put on several hit-lists by black market traders.
 
Together with the help of Yar, a long-time buddy of his from the Army Warehouses, many stalkers find themselves becoming unwitting pawns in his practical jokes.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Freedom
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Axel%c[0,140,140,140] serves as a medic aboard the Skadovsk in Zaton.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Very little is known about Axel as he isn't the most talkative of stalkers, though rumours in circulation would suggest he only came to the Zone to escape from his mother-in-law.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Experienced
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

The %c[0,153,255,255]Barkeep%c[0,140,140,140] is the proprietor of the 100 Rads bar in Rostok.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Due to Rostok's central location in the busier parts of the Zone, the Barkeep is a very important trader - a lifeline for stalkers headed to or returning from battle.
 
Aside from the obvious functions of managing the bar, he rents rooms, pays out bribes, and supplies stalkers with plenty of equipment and work.
 
He is respected greatly by many stalkers as he was instrumental in the opening of roads leading toward the center of the Zone.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Legend
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Beard%c[0,140,140,140] is an artefact dealer and barkeeper aboard the Skadovsk.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
He is a very important figure near the center of the Zone, and effectively the leader of loners in Zaton.
 
Though mainly artefact recovery he does offer various types of work, and he always appears willing to do whatever he sees as in the best interests of his people.
 
His bar is one of the busiest in the Zone - second only to the 100 Rads - and is almost synonymous with the Skadovsk itself.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Arnie%c[0,140,140,140] - stalker, manager of the Arena, an entertainment establishment in the Bar.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Has good relations with the Barkeep, runs a profitable business by organizing fights and taking bets.
 
His main audience and helpers in recruiting participants are from the Duty faction.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Professional
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

%c[0,153,255,255]Cardan%c[0,140,140,140] is an experienced technician and a recovering alcoholic.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
He came to the Zone with his two friends, Joker and Barge, who after a fierce, booze-fuelled argument, decided to go their separate ways.
 
From here he took up residence at the Skadovsk where he proceeded to sink further into his alcoholism, only swearing off drinking when learning of his former friends' demise.
 
Prior to this, he worked on the development of the so-called "Project 62" - a prototype of the Gauss rifle - until its eventual closure in 2004.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Forester%c[0,140,140,140] - an old forester. The only surviving local resident who refused to evacuate even during the first accident at the Chernobyl Nuclear Power Plant in 1986.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Has the ability to sense anomalies without electronic devices. Lives in the abandoned mine structure on the outskirts of the Red Forest.
 
The Forester grew up in one of the villages near Limansk. Sometimes he saw the residents of this town. Became a forester of the "Limansk Reserve", guarding the forest from poachers. After the Chernobyl disaster, the Forester wanted to be evacuated, but he refused because of his attachment to his native places. After the second explosion at the station, he discovered the ability to smell and avoid anomalies. Independently, with the help of the found artifact "Compass", managed to get out of the "space bubble".
 
He is also a technician who repairs and upgrades equipment.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Legend
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Cold%c[0,140,140,140] helped restore and now leads the Clear Sky faction.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Formerly just the barkeeper, Cold is one of few in the Zone to have fallen upwards, taking Clear Sky's reins after most of its key members either died or deserted.
 
Known by his comrades as a cheerful and good-natured person, as well as a lover of vulgar jokes, he came to the Zone because he could not seem to find his place in the outside world.
 
There is little doubt that he made the right decision, restoring his faction to its former glory and now occupying an important seat of power in the Zone.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Clear Sky
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Noah%c[0,140,140,140] - a hermit from an old barge.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
A free stalker living in Zaton on an old barge he calls the "Ark". Noah hates mutants and occasionally conducts sweeps of caves on the southern plateau.
 
Noah meets uninvited guests with shotgun fire, always mistaking them for mutants. Additionally, he suffers from partial memory loss.
 
Noah believes that in the near future, a large wave of mutants will come, which will drive all stalkers out of the Zone, after which a powerful emission will occur, from which you can only be saved in the "Ark".
 
Noah has a tamed pseudo-dog named Lassie.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Professional
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

%c[0,153,255,255]Dushman%c[0,140,140,140] - the leader of the mercenary group in Dead City.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Well-armed and very dangerous. Wears a unique suit made to order. Judging by his appearance, he has a lot of combat experience.
 
According to rumors, Dushman has an ugly, nasty character and he keeps his subordinates on a tight leash. Additionally, he is a trader for the group.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Mercenary
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Bad

%c[0,153,255,255]Hog%c[0,140,140,140] - an experienced technician of the mercenary group in Dead City.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Former commander of a mercenary group at the Army Warehouses. Like most mercenaries, he has extensive combat experience.
 
Requalified as a technician for unknown reasons, but he handles this craft very well.
 
Previously carried out various contracts. He even once executed an order for Monolith fighters.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Mercenary
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

The %c[0,153,255,255]Doctor%c[0,140,140,140] is one of few surviving members of Strelok's group.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
According to popular legend, the Doctor was one of the first stalkers to reach the Wish Granter, with the desire to treat any living creature in the Zone.
 
His wish was granted, giving him the gift of healing, but erasing his distinction between people and mutants.
 
This is denied by the Doctor himself as just a story, saying that none of those who set out to find it have ever returned alive.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner (Trader)
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Legend
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Fanatic%c[0,140,140,140] earns his name from being a dedicated and aggressive stalker.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Following Wolf's departure for the Army Warehouses until his return, Fanatic took charge in looking after new arrivals to the Zone at the rookie village.
 
Despite no-longer being solely in charge he is still regarded as an authority, though he much prefers to be out fighting bandits and mutants than standing idly by.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Brilliant

%c[0,153,255,255]Gonta%c[0,140,140,140] is a professional mutant hunter and a pupil of Trapper.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
On the orders of Trapper he arrived to Zaton with Garmata, Crab, and Danila, to destroy a chimera which had been terrorising stalkers in the vicinity.
 
Right from the beginning their venture was struck by bad luck, with Danila mysteriously disappearing and leaving them a man short, and Crab being left bedridden after suffering injuries.
 
They hired a stalker named Magpie to assist them, who instead backed out at the last moment, stealing their gear and then fleeing for Yanov station.
 
With the help of a mysterious stalker, Gonta killed the chimera, uncovered Danila's fate, and discovered Magpie's whereabouts - delivering him justice soon after.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Hawaiian%c[0,140,140,140] is Yanov station's barkeeper and trader.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Despite what his name would imply he isn't actually from Hawaii, but is nicknamed as such for often exclaiming "Aloha!" when greeting his customers.
 
Almost the entirety of his time is spent working at the bar, where he sells both food and drink, alongside weapons and ammunition.
 
Interestingly, this not only makes him one of only two barkeepers in the Zone who also works as a regular trader - the other being the Barkeep - but also places him within the same building as another trader - Ashot.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Bonesetter%c[0,140,140,140] - medic at Yanov station.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Experienced medic providing services to stalkers in the station's terminal at Yanov.
 
A cheerful person with a light-hearted character.
 
His room is located within Freedom territory, but unlike Nitro, Bonesetter doesn't drop any hints about his personal preferences.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Experienced
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Trapper%c[0,140,140,140] - mutant hunter.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Professional hunter from Yanov station.
 
Entered the Zone together with Stalker Lis. Killed by a chimera, after which Trapper stopped going hunting. Trapper is acquainted with Gonta, for whom he is the highest authority.
 
Resides in the basement of the Yanov railway station.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Nitro%c[0,140,140,140] - technician at Yanov station.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Talented loner technician from Yanov station, one of the creators of the technical workshop services and one of the best technicians in the Zone. Supports the Duty faction.
 
Nitro has an acquaintance named Senka, whom the technician sends on minor tasks.
 
Interested in radio technology.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Professor Ozersky%c[0,140,140,140] - scientist from the mobile laboratory near the Jupiter plant.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Together with Professor Hermann, he studies the flora and fauna of the Zone.
 
Unlike his colleague Hermann, he is more friendly towards some stalkers.
 
Optimistic, believes in the triumph of science.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Ecologist
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Experienced
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Professor Hermann%c[0,140,140,140] - scientist from the mobile laboratory near the Jupiter plant.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Is the immediate supervisor of Ozersky and all residents of the scientist's bunker.
 
Originally worked at Sakharov's laboratory. Later, Hermann and Professor Ozersky were sent to the outskirts of Jupiter during an expedition to study the local flora and fauna of the Zone.
 
Manages the technical and scientific inventory of the expedition.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Ecologist
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Experienced
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Tukarev%c[0,140,140,140] - technician from the mobile laboratory near the Jupiter plant.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Scientific technician who arrived in the Zone to replace Novikov, a technician who briefly worked under contract in this bunker.
 
Looks like an experienced technician specializing exclusively in the improvement of suits and helmets.
 
Not very talkative, but knows his business.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Group: Ecologist
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Experienced
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Ivan Trodnik%c[0,140,140,140] is the former guide, and now medic of Clear Sky.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Once a guide leading his fellow faction members through the unexplored marshes, Ivan now spends his days patching up the wounded.
 
Forced into his position out of necessity rather than by choice, he was Clear Sky's most experienced medic following their near-collapse.
 
Now wishing he had succumb to wounds from his earlier time exploring, his only remaining joys in life are drinking and blaming the mercenary Scar for his troubles.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Clear Sky
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Librarian%c[0,140,140,140] despite his name is actually Clear Sky's barkeeper.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
After the emission that cut the swamps off from the rest of the Zone, Librarian found himself trapped with no easy way out.
 
Facing death at the hands of bandits or worse, he headed to the far southern reaches and joined Clear Sky, where he would later replace Cold as their barkeeper following his promotion to leader.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Clear Sky
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Lieutenant Kirilov%c[0,140,140,140] is one of the military's most gifted technicians.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Originally just a radio operator, Kirilov has always had a knack for taking things apart and putting them back together.
 
After the failure to Operation Fairway, he and his group took refuge in an old laundromat where he harnessed his skills in repairing their equipment and weapons.
 
Following these events he was recommended by Colonel Kovalsky, his commander, to take up the role of being the military's foremost technician at their base in Agroprom.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Military
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Limpid%c[0,140,140,140] is a bandit and long-time technician.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Previously working at a factory as a mechanical engineer, it is fair to say that he is well versed in technology.
 
However after being fired from his job following a drunken brawl, he fell into alcoholism and eventually lost both his wife and his home shortly after.
 
With nothing left in life he sought out the Zone to start over, soon after meeting bandits at the Garbage and finding work as their technician ever since.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Bandit
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Professional
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Loki%c[0,140,140,140] is the leader of Freedom's garrison at Yanov station.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
When the Brain Scorcher was disabled and the rush to the center of the Zone began, Loki was the man put in charge of Freedom's expedition.
 
Unlike other leaders in the faction he takes a less zealous approach toward their ideology, once even agreeing to a ceasefire with Duty.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Freedom
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Brilliant

%c[0,153,255,255]Major Kuznetsov%c[0,140,140,140] is a callous and extremely corrupt military officer.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Since arriving to the Zone only a few years ago, Kuznetsov has risen from being a lowly checkpoint keeper to an experienced commander feared by his subordinates.
 
Despite holding little official power within command-and-control, he has bribed and backstabbed his way to the top where few are willing to oppose him.
 
As a result, he has effectively become the de facto leader of military forces in much of the Zone in all but name.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Military
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

%c[0,153,255,255]Nimble%c[0,140,140,140] is a profiteer who sells smuggled weapons and outfits.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Starting out as a member of Clear Sky, and then as a courier for Sidorovich, Nimble has assembled a list of important contacts allowing him to build his own smuggling business.
 
Unlike other traders in the Zone, he specialises in the sale of rare equipment and items that can be very difficult to obtain, as such he never knows what he'll get his hands on next.
 
He originally set up shop at the Skadovsk in Zaton, but following the resurgence of the Monolith faction has since relocated to the Bar, he sells his wares in a back room.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Legend
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Novikov%c[0,140,140,140], also known as %c[0,153,255,255]Grey%c[0,140,140,140], is Clear Sky's veteran technician.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
A member of the faction since their earliest days, Novikov is described by his comrades as an expert in his field, with Cold saying that he can make a rifle out of an empty can with ammo to boot.
 
After Clear Sky's destruction he briefly left to take up contract work with the Ecologists at the mobile science lab in Jupiter, but later returned after discovering they had pulled through and rebuilt.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Clear Sky
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Brilliant

%c[0,153,255,255]Olivius%c[0,140,140,140] fences loot and other stolen goods for the bandits.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Capitalising on the chaos following the assassination of Borov, the former bandit leader, he quickly entrenched his influence within the Dark Valley.
 
Though few would consider the bandits to currently have a leader, least of all themselves, there is little in the way of information, contracts, or trade involving the faction that Olivius doesn't have his hands in.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Bandit
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Owl%c[0,140,140,140] is a blackmarket trader with questionable loyalties.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
An uncomfortable and rude person, of all the inhabitants aboard the Skadovsk he stands out as a bad character with a grumpy temper.
 
He has a rich assortment of weapons and ammunition, and very much appreciates any information which stalkers are willing to sell to him.
 
Because of his ties to Sultan, and both the manner of his speech and his appearance, many loners believe him to have once been a bandit.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Bad

%c[0,153,255,255]Professor Kruglov%c[0,140,140,140] is a doctor of biological sciences.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Prior to arriving in the Zone, Kruglov studied pathogenic mutations in animals and their effects, so it was only a matter of time until his research brought him here.
 
For the last several years he has worked with his associate, Professor Sakharov, and claims to have made several major discoveries - though few stalkers actually know what he's talking about and usually just feign an understanding.
 
As of late he has taken to running his own research lab in a pod near the main bunker in Yantar, where he offers a variety of technical work to those who visit him.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Ecologist
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Experienced
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Bad

%c[0,153,255,255]Professor Sakharov%c[0,140,140,140] is one of the Zone's most veteran researchers.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
As the chief scientist at Yantar's mobile science lab, Sakharov has shared involvement with the majority of scientific breakthroughs to have originated from the Zone.
 
Although seen as polite and professional, his only true care is for scientific discoveries, rarely appearing to spare any thought for his researchers when sending them to certain death.
 
Because of the nature of his work he has an almost limitless supply of contracts to recover artefacts and mutant parts, making him a very popular employer amongst foolhardy stalkers.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Ecologist (Trader)
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Brilliant

%c[0,153,255,255]Sergeant Spooner%c[0,140,140,140] is an expert soldier and a cunning merchant.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Throughout much of his life information has always played an important factor in his decision making, choosing carefully what he shares and to whom he shares it with.
 
Naturally he found his place after joining the military where his skills are of great value, and now uses his position as a front for his personal trading and information broking business.
 
Very little information is otherwise known about him, least of all as to why he has such a peculiar nickname.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Military
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Sidorovich%c[0,140,140,140] trades out of an old bunker near the rookie village.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
He enjoys a reputation of being a fairly honest businessman who gives newcomers a chance to survive, whilst his wealth of contacts allows him to smuggle in luxury goods.
 
However many also see him as greedy and tight-fisted. Those who try to compete with him on his own territory, or just disgruntle him in general, often end up on the wrong side of an assassination contract.
 
Whether for good or bad, he continues to play an integral role within the Zone's economy and its factions' politics, as such he is a great source of work for those wanting to earn a quick payday.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner (Trader)
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Legend
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Spore%c[0,140,140,140] is Clear Sky's trader and Nimble's trusted ally.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Like all stalkers before him Spore came to the Zone seeking his fortune, immediately setting to work smuggling goods and information to, from, and through the Zone.
 
Before long he caught the ears of Nimble who soon after requested a meet, and the two immediately realised the potency of combining their efforts.
 
Through Nimble's contacts within his former faction, Spore was soon installed as Clear Sky's trader, allowing them to secure smuggling routes through the marshes and quickly establish what would become a thriving business relationship.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Clear Sky
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Brilliant

%c[0,153,255,255]Sultan%c[0,140,140,140] is the former leader of bandits in Zaton.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Considering his adoption of an eastern ruler's title for his name, many in the Zone consider Sultan to be a man who thinks very highly of himself.
 
His life has been one full of trouble; following the death of a close friend at the hands of substance abuse, Sultan killed the dealers responsible and was later imprisoned.
 
Following his release he soon enough found himself imprisoned yet again because of his "business", eventually leaving for the Zone where he joined the bandits, climbed the career ladder among thieves, and began to run his gang.
 
Ultimately all of his attempts to establish himself as a leader within the faction were met with failure, as his enterprises and dealings within Zaton were crushed and swept away by Beard's loners.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Bandit
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Terrible

%c[0,153,255,255]Wolf%c[0,140,140,140] leads the defence of the rookie village.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Tasking himself with protecting the village and the stalkers that live there from outside threats, Wolf uses a network of scouts and spies to keep track of unfriendly movements that occur within the Cordon.
 
Sharing information he learns with Sidorovich, they frequently send out raiding parties and opportunists to clean out encroaching bandits, mutants, and other threats to their mutual interest in the village's security.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Legend
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Xenotech%c[0,140,140,140] is a technician who repairs gear mostly for newbie stalkers.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
Working out of the rookie village in the Cordon - Xenotech is a gifted technician whose efforts are of great value to stalkers new to the Zone.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Loner
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Professional
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Good

%c[0,153,255,255]Yar%c[0,140,140,140], also known as %c[0,153,255,255]Uncle Yar%c[0,140,140,140], is a stalker with many talents.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information%c[0,140,140,140] 
He is well known in the Zone for being a brilliant technician, with jokes that he can make an anti-aircraft gun from a rusty rifle perhaps not being all that far from the truth.
 
Following the end of the Faction Wars and the rush towards the center of the Zone, he travelled with Freedom's expedition to Yanov station, before very briefly working as a medic in the Outskirts.
 
He soon returned after the ceasefire with Duty broke down, where he decided yet again to change his profession and try his hand at becoming an information broker.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Statistics%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Freedom
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]General Voronin%c[0,140,140,140] - the leader of the Duty faction.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
The leader of the Duty faction since 2012. Successor to General Krylov.
 
After General Krylov's departure at the end of 2011 or beginning of 2012, he took control.
 
Voronin pursues a policy of armed neutrality with the Freedom faction.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Duty
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Colonel Petrenko%c[0,140,140,140] - a trader of the Duty faction.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
One of the founders of Duty and, concurrently, the chief ideologist.
 
He developed the code of rules and recruited almost all key members of Duty.
 
Supplier of the Duty faction, deputy to General Voronin.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Duty
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

%c[0,153,255,255]Mangun%c[0,140,140,140] - an experienced mechanic of the Duty faction.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
A reserved and calm technician of Duty.
 
He likes to take a couple of shots but doesn't lose his grip, even offering a small discount.
 
Mangun is highly valued in Duty.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Duty
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Experienced
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

%c[0,153,255,255]Lukash%c[0,140,140,140] - the leader of the Freedom faction at the Army Warehouses.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Lukash is the successor to Mikhula, the former leader of the Freedom clan, and accordingly, he took his place.
 
This happened in 2012, when Mikhula, secretly gathering a squad, went to the Burner - Since there was no word or spirit from Mikhula, Lukash decided to take control of the faction into his own hands.
 
Under Lukash, the faction became even stronger.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Freedom
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Excellent

%c[0,153,255,255]Skinflint%c[0,140,140,140] - a trader of the Freedom faction at the Army Warehouses.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Member of the Freedom faction. Deputy to Lukash (faction leader), warehouse manager, and trader at the Freedom base at the Army Warehouses.
 
Known for his stingy nature.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Freedom
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Brilliant

%c[0,153,255,255]Screw%c[0,140,140,140] - a technician of the Freedom faction at the Army Warehouses.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
A well-known mechanic in the Zone. Can turn a tractor into a tank.
 
Tries to repair vehicles at the base.
 
Also fixes items for his "comrades," whether it's weapons or armor.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Freedom
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Veteran
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Great

%c[0,153,255,255]Rabbit%c[0,140,140,140] - the leader of the Monolith faction in the center of Pripyat.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Besides being the leader, he is also a trader of the faction. Nothing else is known about this man.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Monolith
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Master
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Neutral

%c[0,153,255,255]Cleric%c[0,140,140,140] - a mechanic of the Monolith faction in the center of Pripyat.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Information:%c[0,140,140,140] 
Looks more like a very experienced fighter than a mechanic, but his hands are in the right place. He is also the owner of an extremely rare version of the Gauss gun.
 
%c[0,0,255,30]Data:%c[0,140,140,140] 
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Faction: Monolith
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Rank: Expert
%c[0,200,0,255]• %c[0,140,140,140]Reputation: Bad
